hypothesis: Mutating position 5 to aromatic residues (W/F/Y) will enhance SSTR2-specific TM5/ECL2 complementarity, while mutating position 11 to hydrophobic residues (L/I/V) will reduce off-target SSTR3/SSTR5 interactions without compromising SSTR2 binding.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current hypothesis is that SSTR2 selectivity can be achieved by optimizing non-pharmacophore positions to disrupt off-target interactions. This iteration supports the hypothesis but highlights the need for more aggressive mutation of positions 1, 2, 5, 6, 11, and 12.

proposed_changes.rationale: To improve SSTR2 selectivity, we must disrupt conserved pocket contacts in off-targets SSTR3/SSTR5. These positions are non-pharmacophore and can be mutated to reduce off-target binding without affecting the core pharmacophore (FWKT(7-10) and Cys3/Cys14).

These mutations are designed to disrupt conserved hydrophobic or hydrogen-bonding interactions in off-target SSTR3/SSTR5 pockets. Aromatic or hydrophobic residues at these positions may contribute to off-target binding.
